title: The Mystery of Amelia Earhart's Disappearance
summary: Amelia Earhart was a famous pilot who tried to fly around the world in 1937 but disappeared over the ocean. People have many ideas about what happened to her, like crashing into the sea or landing on an island. A new book by Rachel Hartigan looks at these ideas and tells about Earhart's life. Hartigan went on a trip to an island to search for clues but did not find proof. She thinks the most likely story is that Earhart got lost and ran out of gas. The book helps us understand why people are still curious about this mystery today.

background_read: Amelia Earhart was a brave pilot in the 1930s who wanted to be the first woman to fly around the world. She set off on a big adventure but never returned, leaving a mystery that has lasted for many years. People have looked for her plane and clues on islands, but no one knows for sure what happened. This story shows how explorers can inspire others and why we like to solve puzzles from history.
Article_Structure: WHO: Amelia Earhart, a famous pilot, and Rachel Hartigan, an author who wrote a book about her.
WHAT: A book that explores theories about Earhart's disappearance and her life story.
WHERE: Over the ocean and on Nikumaroro Island, where searches happened.
WHY: To understand the mystery of what happened to Earhart and share her inspiring life.

perspectives.description: Some people enjoy trying to solve the puzzle of Earhart's disappearance by looking for clues and evidence.
